notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for slovenia. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

chunks.title: Chinese passport tourism route for Slovenia
chunks.content: Chinese ordinary passport holders planning a short tourism trip to Slovenia should normally use the Slovenia/Schengen short-stay Type C tourism route when Slovenia is the only destination, the longest-stay destination, or the main purpose destination. VIZA AI should first confirm nationality, legal residence, destination set, stay length, day split across Schengen countries, first entry if stays are equal, purpose, passport validity, travel medical insurance, accommodation, itinerary, funds, return ties and biometrics. Do not route this trip to US, UK, Vietnam, Indonesia or another non-Schengen visa.

chunks.title: Slovenia Schengen where-to-apply and documents
chunks.content: EU Schengen guidance says a short-stay Schengen visa covers temporary visits of up to 90 days in any 180-day period. For a single-country trip, apply to the consulate/official visa channel of that country. For multiple Schengen countries, apply to the country where the traveller will spend the longest time or, if stays are equal, the first-entry country. Applications should normally be submitted at least 15 days before travel and no earlier than 6 months before the intended journey. Common documents include passport, signed Schengen application form, ICAO-compliant photo, travel medical insurance, itinerary/accommodation, funds, evidence of return intention, and fingerprints. EU guidance lists the adult visa fee as EUR 90 and normal processing as 15 days, extendable up to 45 days. Use Slovenia's official consular or outsourced visa application channel for the applicant's legal residence country before booking an appointment. EU common source: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en

documents.title: Slovenia Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Slovenia application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use the competent Slovenia consulate or its authorised visa application centre when Slovenia is the only destination, main destination, longest-stay destination, or first-entry state when stays are equal. Also check the country-specific source: https://www.gov.si/en/topics/entry-and-residence/. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf ; https://www.gov.si/en/topics/entry-and-residence/. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Slovenia fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: The harmonised Schengen form intake should collect surname/current and birth names, first names, date/place/country of birth, current and birth nationality, sex, civil status, parental authority for minors, national ID where applicable, travel document type/number/issue/expiry/issuing authority, home address/email/phone, residence in another country if any, occupation, employer or school, travel purpose, destination Member State(s), first-entry Member State, number of entries requested, intended arrival/departure dates and duration, prior Schengen fingerprints, entry permit for final destination if any, inviting person/company or hotel/accommodation, who pays travel/living costs, EU/EEA/Swiss family member data if applicable, and applicant declaration/signature.

chunks.title: Slovenia supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Core Schengen evidence includes signed visa application form, passport/travel document valid beyond the planned Schengen exit, photo, travel medical insurance, proof of purpose, itinerary/transport, accommodation or host invitation, evidence of financial means, evidence of intention to return, residence/jurisdiction proof, fee, and biometrics where required. Workflow guardrails: VIZA should determine the competent Schengen state before filling forms. For multi-country trips, compute longest stay or first entry if equal; collect day split and accommodation per country. Do not submit or sign declarations without explicit user confirmation.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.

chunks.title: Official-source field answer norms
chunks.content:
Official-source field answer norms for slovenia.
Use these norms only as filling guidance; if a live official form, passport, or uploaded document shows a more specific value, that document controls the answer.

Crawled source URLs: https://www.gov.si/en/topics/entry-and-residence/ ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf

Passport and identity fields:
- Entry is permitted on the basis of a valid identity card or a valid passport, irrespective of the reason for entering or the reason for residing in the country – employment, study, self-employment, relocation, etc.
- an individual whom the EU citizen or Slovenian citizen is obliged to maintain or actually maintains in accordance with the acts of his or her country of citizenship,– children above the age of 21 and ancestors whom the EU citizen or the Slovenian citizen is obliged to maintain or actually maintains in accordance with the legislation of the state of which he 
- children above the age of 21 and older relatives whom an EU citizen is obliged to maintain or actually maintains in accordance with the acts of his or her country of citizenship,

Issue, expiry, and validity fields:
- However, for a longer stay, EU citizens must obtain a residence registration certificate at the administrative unit in their area of residence before the expiry of the authorised three-month stay.
- EU citizens who wish to reside in the Republic of Slovenia for a period longer than three months may apply for a residence registration certificate at the administrative unit in their area of residence immediately upon their entry into the country or before the expiry of the authorised three-month stay.
- For a longer stay, they must apply for a residence permit for family members prior to the expiration date of the authorised 90-day stay.

Travel plan and port fields:
- The methods and conditions of entry into the Republic of Slovenia differ depending on whether the person entering the country is a citizen of a Member State of the European Union, or a third-country national.
- Entry is permitted on the basis of a valid identity card or a valid passport, irrespective of the reason for entering or the reason for residing in the country – employment, study, self-employment, relocation, etc.
- For the first three months after entry, he or she may reside in Slovenia without residence registration.

Accommodation, host, and address fields:
- In the visa application procedure, the applicant must also submit a letter of guarantee (available in the Form below), which has been certified and registered at the relevant administrative unit in Slovenia or an Invitation letter.
- The Letter of guarantee must be used in cases the guarantor provides accommodation or means of subsistance both for the duration of the intended stay and for the return to his country of origin or residence.
- The form of Invitation letter is not prescribed, but its content must comply with the requirements of Article 24 of the Act on Foreigners.

Purpose, occupation, work, and study fields:
- Entry is permitted on the basis of a valid identity card or a valid passport, irrespective of the reason for entering or the reason for residing in the country – employment, study, self-employment, relocation, etc.
- employment or work, self-employment, provision of services, job search,
- study, vocational training or other forms of education,

Funds, insurance, photo, and supporting-document fields:
- The application must be supported by documents proving that the applicant has health insurance, sufficient economic resources and no criminal conviction in their home country.
- If the EU citizen works, is self-employed or provides services in Slovenia, the requirements to submit proof of health insurance or sufficient economic resources do not apply.
- Proof means evidence of enrolment in an educational programme from an educational establishment or other organisation authorised by the state, or a certificate from the state authority implementing the international or bilateral treaty under which the EU citizen is attending an educational programme in the Republic of Slovenia.

Answering rule: for closed-list fields, prefer the exact official option label/value. For document-backed fields, copy the passport, official identity document, application account, itinerary, invitation, accommodation, insurance, funds, or uploaded support document rather than guessing from a city, country, or prior answer.
